description: Personalised planning with embedded constraints. Checkable minimum bar: full week-by-week structure, engages with actual target pace, includes long runs, a taper and recovery weeks, and works around the fixed Tuesday club session. Physiological sanity of the progression (mileage jumps, hardest sessions placement): read results.jsonl.
prompt: Build me a marathon training plan. I'm 42, my current PB is 3:58 and I want to go under 3:45 at the Manchester Marathon, which is 16 weeks away. I currently run about 25 miles a week and can train 4 days a week at most — one of those is my running club's Tuesday evening session, which I don't want to give up. Lay the plan out week by week with headings 'Week 1' through 'Week 16', state the weekly mileage, and tell me what target race pace I need per mile.

checks.label: states a concrete pace (min/mile or min/km)
works around the Tuesday club session

criteria: Training load is physiologically sensible: progresses from the stated 25 miles/week without dangerous jumps, includes cutback weeks
Race pace arithmetic is correct (sub-3:45 is about 8:34 per mile) and paces for different session types are consistent with it
Respects the constraints: 4 running days maximum with the Tuesday club session used intelligently
Sessions have purpose and variety (long runs building toward race demands, tempo/intervals, easy running easy)
